entry.title: The Master Theorem(主定理)
entry.content:
The Master Theorem

Theorem 4.1 (Master theorem)

Let $a\geq 1$ and $b \geq 1$ be constants, let $f(n)$ be a function, and let $T(n)$ be defined
on the nonnegative integers by the recurrence
$$
T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n)
$$
where we interpret $n/b$ to mean either $\lfloor n/b \rfloor$ or $\lceil n/b \rceil$. Then $T(n$ has the following asymptotic bounds:

  1. If $f(n) = O(n^{\log_{b}(a-\epsilon)})$ for some constant $\epsilon > 0$, then $T(n) = \Theta(n^{\log_{b}{a}})$.
  2. If $f(n) = \Theta(n^{\log_{b}{a}})$, then $T(n) = \Theta(n^{\log_{b}{a}}\lg{n})$.
  3. If $f(n) = \Omega(n^{\log_{b}{(a + \epsilon)}})$ for some constant $\epsilon > 0$, and if $af(n/b) \leq cf(n)$ for some constant $c<1$ and all sufficiently large $n$, then $T(n) = \Theta(f(n))$.

The master method

entry.title: Test
entry.content:
This blog is for test the website

Title 1

Title 2

This is a paragraph, thisis bold, italic, underline and strikeout.

fork me on github

Tables Are Cool
col 3 is right-aligned $1600
col 2 is centered $12
zebra stripes are neat $1

Here is inline code.

Here is block code

Here is a saperate line

Test for mathjax

$\alpha$, $\beta$, $\gamma$

$\Gamma$, $\Lambda$, $\Omega$

$\pm$, $\bigtriangleup$, $\bigcirc$

$\leq$, $\equiv$, $\neq$

$$
x_{1, 2} = \dfrac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^{2} - 4ac}}{2a}
$$

$$
\int_{a}^{b}{f(x)dx} = f(\xi)(b-a)
$$

$$
f(a) = \dfrac{1}{2\pi i}\oint_{\gamma}{\dfrac{f(z)}{z-a}dz}
$$
